relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: These words are given so stumbling does not rule them.
Exclusion and violence will even be mistaken for service to the Divine.
Such action comes from not knowing Father or Son.
Forewarning becomes memory when the hour arrives.
He goes to the Sender, while their questions collapse under grief.
His words fill their heart with grief.
His going opens the sending of the Advocate.
The Advocate exposes the world-order concerning misalignment, justice, and judgment.
Misalignment is revealed as refusal of trust.
Justice is revealed in his going to the Father.
Judgment is revealed because the ruler of this world-order is judged.
More remains, but they cannot yet bear it.
The Spirit of truth will guide into all truth.
The Spirit glorifies Jesus by receiving and announcing what is his.
All of the Father’s is shared with the Son.
A little while holds absence and seeing together.
The disciples puzzle over little while and Father-going.
They cannot grasp the phrase.
Jesus knows the question beneath their words.
Grief will become joy while the world rejoices wrongly.
Birth grief becomes joy over new life.
Their heart will rejoice with joy no one can take.
That day opens asking the Father in the name.
Ask and receive so joy may be fulfilled.
Figures will give way to plain Father-speech.
The name becomes direct asking.
The Father himself loves those who love and trust the Son.
He comes from the Father into the world and returns to the Father.
The disciples think plain speech has arrived.
They confess trust from his knowing.
Jesus questions the strength of their present trust.
They will scatter, yet he is not alone because the Father is with him.
In him is peace; in the world-order pressure; courage comes because he has overcome the world.
